title: Building your own pack
text: pack declares packages single flat file: pack carries helper functions hooks must use nested layout, sidecar files functions/ directory next manifest: manifest file name, directory name, name field must match. Declaring functions [hooks] flat pack validation error.

title: Building your own pack
text: Required fields: name — must equal manifest file name (without .toml). description — one-line string. packages — always required, even empty (packages = []). Optional fields: [sources] — per-package install specs (e.g. "ggtext" = "wilkelab/ggtext") packages install plain CRAN name. attach — startup attachment intent: omit true attach pack’s packages, false install without attaching, character vector attach subset. extends — names packs pack builds ; packages, functions, hooks resolve transitively. functions — helper functions pack ships. Every listed name must matching functions/fn_<name>.R sidecar file. [hooks] on_add — functions run automatically pack first added project. [settings] — default values pack settings project can override; see “Pack settings” . fn_<name>.R sidecar copied project’s boosters/ folder add_pack() sourced global environment startup via .Rprofile hook. Sidecars ordinary R files: define function (object) named nothing else surprising, since sourced every session.
code: name = "scaffold-analysis" description = "Create a compact analysis project folder scaffold." packages = ["fs", "here"] functions = ["scaffold_analysis"]  [hooks] on_add = ["scaffold_analysis"]

title: Building your own pack
text: on_add turns pack “install things” “set project ”. scaffold-analysis pack added, boosterpak copies sources fn_scaffold_analysis.R, calls scaffold_analysis(), builds analysis folder tree. rules, precisely: Hooks run , genuinely new add. hook fires pack already listed [packs].declared. Re-adding declared pack nothing. Hooks require sync = TRUE (default). add_pack(name, sync = FALSE) declares pack copies functions skips hook entirely — later catch-. sync() never runs hooks. Syncing rematerializes missing function files installs packages, re-run (first-run) on_add hook. skipped hook add time, call function . Hooks called arguments. configuration must live function’s defaults. need different behavior (say, different folder list scaffold_analysis()), edit sidecar file pack — pack’s copy source truth. Hooks run project root. boosterpak temporarily sets working directory project root, relative paths hook resolve project, wherever happened call add_pack() . hook must one pack’s functions. Validation rejects manifest whose on_add names anything listed functions, hook looked global environment sourcing — pack run code ship. Ordering: functions copied sourced first, sync() installs packages, hooks run. hook can therefore rely pack’s packages installed, still guard requireNamespace() clear error installation skipped. GitHub packs behave identically. add_github_pack() runs on_add hooks conditions: new add plus sync = TRUE. common surprise second rule: add pack sync = FALSE, pack name already declared boosters.toml (example hand-editing file cloning project), hook never fires. cloned project intentional — sync() restores environment without re-running project-scaffolding side effects.

title: Building your own pack
text: Hooks take arguments, [settings] table pack exposes knobs vary per project. pack declares defaults: first time pack added project, defaults copied [settings.packs.<name>] section appended boosters.toml (packs pulled via extends get sections ): Edit section freely — per-project override. Sidecar functions read effective value pack_setting(), resolves project override → pack default → default argument: rules mirror on_add hooks: Scaffolded , never overwritten. section appended header missing. Re-adding pack, sync(), every boosterpak write leave edits alone — writers touch existing boosters.toml either rewrite single known line append, custom sections survive construction. First add , regardless sync. Unlike hooks, scaffolding also happens add_pack(name, sync = FALSE). Values loosely typed — strings, booleans, numbers, string arrays. consuming function validates reads. remove_pack() keeps section, way keeps edited fn_*.R files. Delete hand longer want .
code: name = "targets-curation" description = "Scaffold a targets-based curation pipeline." packages = ["targets", "tarchetypes", "cli"] functions = ["scaffold_targets_curation"]  [settings] dirs = ["data/raw", "data/processed", "R"]  [hooks] on_add = ["scaffold_targets_curation"] [settings.packs.targets-curation] dirs = ["data/raw", "data/processed", "R"] scaffold_targets_curation <- function(   dirs = boosterpak::pack_setting(     "targets-curation",     "dirs",     default = c("data/raw", "R")   ) ) {   fs::dir_create(fs::path(getwd(), dirs)) }

title: Building your own pack
text: Packs resolve three scopes, first match wins: Project — boosters/packs/ project, created save_pack(). User — boosterpak config directory, populated promote_pack(), shared across projects. Built-— shipped package (core, eda, scaffold-analysis, example, github-example). usual authoring loop : get project working, save_pack("name") capture resolved packages helper functions project pack, edit generated manifest (add attach, [sources], [hooks] hand), promote_pack("name") worth reusing elsewhere. See getting started vignette workflow end--end.

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: practice, initialize , add pack function capability intent time, run sync() whenever project match intent, save promote pack setup worth reusing. pack deliberately typed reviewable. Today means packages, optional [sources], optional attach, optional copied helper functions, optional extension packs. arbitrary file sprinkler; project-specific mess stays project, reusable setup invariants belong pack.
code: flowchart TD   A([Start or clone project]) --> B(["Run init()"])   B --> C([Declare reusable capability])   C --> D(["Run add_pack()"])   C --> E(["Run add_function()"])   D --> F(["Run sync()"])   E --> F   F --> G[Hydrate from local libraries]   G --> H[Install remaining with pak]   H --> A1[Write boosters/attach.R]   A1 --> I[Snapshot declared packages]   F --> J[Helper files copied into boosters]   I --> K([Edit code and helper files])   J --> K   K --> L(["Run save_pack()"])   L --> M[Flat package pack or nested function pack]   M --> N(["Run promote_pack()"])   N --> O(["Reuse with add_pack() in another project"])   O --> D    classDef userAction fill:#e8f3ff,stroke:#1f6feb,color:#0b1f3a;   classDef packageAction fill:#f6f8fa,stroke:#6e7781,color:#24292f;   class A,B,C,D,E,F,K,L,N,O userAction;   class G,H,A1,I,J,M packageAction;

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: creates: boosters.toml boosters/packs/ air.toml, enabled settings .Rprofile startup hook, requested generated boosters.toml includes boosterpak [extras].declared, package needed run sync() also captured project intent project lockfile. renv = "yes", init() bootstraps renv, pak, boosterpak project library creates initial lockfile auto_snapshot = true. non-interactive sessions, use explicit renv rprofile arguments. default "ask" mode never silently changes .Rprofile user confirmation required.

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: Apply mode additive. hydrates plain-name missing packages renv-discoverable local libraries, installs anything still missing pak, materializes missing bundled installed helper functions, , auto_snapshot = true, writes boosters/attach.R snapshots renv. Use hydrate = FALSE skip local reuse stricter first-run installs.

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: example pack installs cli. add_pack() preserves unrelated comments sections boosters.toml updating [packs].declared. analysis baseline fs, , janitor, rio, core tidyverse packages, scales, glue, digest, skimr, bundled helper functions, add eda pack explicitly: pack carries helper functions, add_pack() also copies bundled fn_*.R files boosters/ refuses overwrite existing function files unless overwrite_functions = TRUE. Packs can mix ordinary CRAN package names package-specific source specs. Declare every package packages, use [sources] packages install another source: , ggplot2 patchwork can hydrate install package name, ggtext skips hydration uses GitHub source spec. Package attachment startup layer, installation intent. Missing attach means pack attaches direct packages; use attach = true explicit default, attach = false install without startup attachment, attach = ["pkg1", "pkg2"] attach subset. top-level [attach] table supports enabled, declared, exclude. Workflow packages core [extras], including pak, renv, boosterpak, installed attached unless explicitly listed [attach].declared.
code: boosterpak::add_pack("example") boosterpak::add_pack("eda") name = "plotting" description = "Plotting packages from CRAN and GitHub." packages = ["ggplot2", "patchwork", "ggtext"]  [sources] "ggtext" = "wilkelab/ggtext"

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: Helper functions materialized boosters/fn_*.R files tracked [functions].installed. ordinary project files, can edit materialization. sync() restores missing installed helper files, overwrite local edits.

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: save_pack() captures resolved package set , default, helper functions listed [functions].installed. Use functions = "" capture every boosters/fn_*.R file, functions = "none" flat package-pack, character vector specific function names. promote_pack() demote_pack() copy flat package-packs single files nested function-bearing packs whole directories.

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: boosterpak intentionally narrow: pak resolves installs package specs. renv owns project libraries, lockfiles, exact version restoration. boosterpak owns reusable project capability packs: dependencies plus small helper files, attachment choices, setup conventions project-shaped package reusable copy-paste. boundary point. boosterpak uses TOML pack project config, trying general project requirements format.

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: Restore mode requires boosters.toml renv.lock, calls renv::restore(), validates project config, warns direct declared packages absent lockfile. remains lockfile-exact hydrate.

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: project initialized boosterpak 0.5 , restart, boosterpak longer found, run project without relying boosterpak loadable: restart R run:
code: install.packages("renv") renv::install(c("pak", "seanthimons/boosterpak")) renv::snapshot(packages = c("renv", "pak", "boosterpak"), prompt = FALSE) boosterpak::sync()

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: status() reports whether project config exists validates, packs packages resolve, whether project-local renv appears active, whether renv.lock exists, whether boosters/attach.R exists, whether .Rprofile contains startup hook.

code: @Manual{,   title = {boosterpak: Reusable Project Capability Packs},   author = {Sean Thimons},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.6.0.9000},   url = {https://seanthimons.github.io/boosterpak/}, }

title: Reusable Project Capability Packs
text: boosterpak installs reusable R project packs: typed bundles package dependencies, optional source specs, startup attachment intent, helper-function sidecars make project usable without turning package. less package, fistful copied files. boosters.toml project-local control file packs. general dependency-manifest standard boosterpak depend one. pak remains installer, renv remains lockfile project-library layer, boosterpak sits small capability-bundle layer reusable setup conventions. Use boosterpak reusable thing just “packages”, “packages plus helper functions, attachment choices, setup conventions make kind project ready work .”

title: Reusable Project Capability Packs
text: init() writes boosters.toml, creates boosters/packs/, optionally writes air.toml, manages recommended .Rprofile startup hook, can initialize project-local renv. renv = "yes", bootstraps renv, pak, boosterpak project library snapshots workflow packages restart needed.

title: Reusable Project Capability Packs
text: usual loop initialize , add packs helper functions project capability intent, run sync(), capture useful baseline save_pack(). Additive installs hydrate plain-name packages local/user libraries downloading, install remaining packages pak, write boosters/attach.R startup library() calls. Package-packs stay flat boosters/packs/<name>.toml; packs carry copied helper files use boosters/packs/<name>/<name>.toml plus boosters/packs/<name>/functions/. pack deliberately typed reviewable. Today means packages, optional [sources], optional attach, optional copied helper functions, optional extension packs. arbitrary file sprinkler; project-specific mess stays project, reusable setup invariants belong pack.
code: flowchart TD   A([Start or clone project]) --> B(["Run init()"])   B --> C([Declare reusable capability])   C --> D(["Run add_pack()"])   C --> E(["Run add_function()"])   D --> F(["Run sync()"])   E --> F   F --> G[Hydrate from local libraries]   G --> H[Install remaining with pak]   H --> A1[Write boosters/attach.R]   A1 --> I[Snapshot declared packages]   F --> J[Helper files copied into boosters]   I --> K([Edit code and helper files])   J --> K   K --> L(["Run save_pack()"])   L --> M[Flat package pack or nested function pack]   M --> N(["Run promote_pack()"])   N --> O(["Reuse with add_pack() in another project"])   O --> D    classDef userAction fill:#e8f3ff,stroke:#1f6feb,color:#0b1f3a;   classDef packageAction fill:#f6f8fa,stroke:#6e7781,color:#24292f;   class A,B,C,D,E,F,K,L,N,O userAction;   class G,H,A1,I,J,M packageAction;

title: Reusable Project Capability Packs
text: built-pack catalog contains: core: minimal bootstrap dependencies, pak renv. eda: analysis helpers including fs, , janitor, rio, core tidyverse packages, scales, glue, digest, skimr, bundled helper functions. example: small documented example installs cli. scaffold-analysis: installs fs carries helper compact analysis folder scaffold. github-example: installs ComptoxR seanthimons/ComptoxR. Packs can mix ordinary CRAN package names source-specific install specs. Declare every package packages, add [sources] entry packages come somewhere else: pack, ggplot2 patchwork install package name, ggtext uses GitHub source spec. Attachment separate installation. Missing attach means pack attaches direct packages; packs can also use attach = true, attach = false, attach = ["pkg1", "pkg2"]. top-level [attach] table can add packages declared, remove packages exclude, set enabled = false remove managed boosters/attach.R file. Workflow packages core [extras], pak, renv, boosterpak, installed attached unless explicitly listed [attach].declared. Pack mutation additive. Removing pack updates boosters.toml can run sync, uninstall packages. default, add_pack() sync(mode = "apply") use hydrate = TRUE, ordinary CRAN-style package names can copied renv-discoverable local libraries pak downloads anything. Source-specific declarations, GitHub remotes [sources], skip hydration install declared spec. Use hydrate = FALSE stricter first-run installs go straight pak.
code: boosterpak::add_pack("example") name = "plotting" description = "Plotting packages from CRAN and GitHub." packages = ["ggplot2", "patchwork", "ggtext"]  [sources] "ggtext" = "wilkelab/ggtext"

title: Reusable Project Capability Packs
text: create_pack() writes new pack declared intent without adding boosters.toml, installing packages, running sync(). Plain package names written directly packages; source-specific specs preserved [sources]. Use function_template = "yes" create nested pack layout functions/fn_template.R later manual helper authoring. save_pack() writes TOML snapshot currently resolved project packages , default, helper functions listed [functions].installed. Use functions = "" capture every boosters/fn_*.R file, functions = "none" flat package-pack, = "core" fork one existing pack, scope = "user" write directly machine-wide user pack directory. promote_pack() demote_pack() copy flat package-packs single files nested function-bearing packs whole directories.

title: Reusable Project Capability Packs
text: boosterpak intentionally narrow: pak resolves installs package specs. renv owns project libraries, lockfiles, exact version restoration. boosterpak owns reusable project capability packs: dependencies plus small helper files, attachment choices, setup conventions project-shaped package reusable copy-paste. boundary point. boosterpak uses TOML pack project config, trying general project requirements format.

title: Reusable Project Capability Packs
text: sync(mode = "apply") treats boosters.toml reusable setup intent renv.lock downstream output. may hydrate local libraries installing remaining declared packages pak, writes boosters/attach.R snapshot. sync(mode = "restore") explicit path exact lockfile restoration hydrate.

title: Reusable Project Capability Packs
text: project initialized boosterpak 0.5 restart made boosterpak unavailable inside renv, run without relying boosterpak loadable: restart R project run:
code: install.packages("renv") renv::install(c("pak", "seanthimons/boosterpak")) renv::snapshot(packages = c("renv", "pak", "boosterpak"), prompt = FALSE) boosterpak::sync()

title: Reusable Project Capability Packs
text: status() reports config validity, declared resolved packs, package counts, missing direct packages, attach package count file presence, function drift missing materialized files, pack catalog counts, renv state, lockfile presence, .Rprofile hook. Current development includes function materialization, pack capture/promotion, broader project status reporting; pruning remains scope.

title: boosterpak 0.6.0.9000
text: Documentation now frames boosterpak reusable project capability packs: dependencies plus helper files, attachment choices, setup conventions. package uses TOML config, general dependency-manifest standard. Added add_github_pack() import pack manifests GitHub repository git URL project-local boosters/packs/, declare selected packs, optionally run existing sync flow. Added create_pack() guided pack authoring declared package set. writes pack TOML, optional source specs, explicit attach intent, optional nested function-template sidecar without mutating boosters.toml running sync(). sync(mode = "apply") now writes managed boosters/attach.R file static library() calls startup attachment intent. Packs can declare attach = true, attach = false, attach = ["pkg"]; missing attach attaches direct pack packages default. New top-level [attach] config supports enabled, declared, exclude. Workflow packages core [extras] installed attached unless explicitly listed [attach].declared. recommended .Rprofile hook now sources boosters/attach.R boosters/fn_*.R helper files, status() reports attach state.

title: boosterpak 0.5.0.9001
text: sync(mode = "apply") eager add_pack() now hydrate plain-name missing packages renv-discoverable local libraries falling back pak::pkg_install(). Use hydrate = FALSE skip local reuse; sync(mode = "restore") remains exact lockfile restore path.

title: boosterpak 0.5.0.9000
text: init(renv = "yes") now initializes loads renv without immediate restart, installs bootstrap workflow packages pak, renv, boosterpak project library, snapshots auto_snapshot = true. built-core pack now minimal (pak, renv). previous analysis-oriented package set available new eda pack. Projects initialized boosterpak 0.5 can recover running: restart R run boosterpak::sync().
code: install.packages("renv") renv::install(c("pak", "seanthimons/boosterpak")) renv::snapshot(packages = c("renv", "pak", "boosterpak"), prompt = FALSE)
